finding_results.notes: Added three new Carrow speech acts to the corridor torture scene. Line 49: two-sentence block between curses — 'Told you about that, didn't I. Told you last week.' followed by 'You're carrying that round my corridor like I won't notice. You think I'm thick?' — establishing his repetitive scolding register and insecure-bully threat-question pattern with high contraction use and colloquial 'round my corridor.' Line 75: post-curse dismissal 'Pick it up and go' while already scanning the corridor, showing the banal transactional quality of his cruelty. Combined with the existing 'Get up' at line 71, the scene now carries four Carrow speech acts total. All new lines use contractions, contain imperatives or threat questions, and are unmistakably Carrow's blunt working-class register overlaid with petty authority — distinct from Gavin, Miren, and Babbling. No villain-monologue grandeur, no speechifying, no profanity beyond the level warranted by the scene's casual cruelty. The boy's inaudible response (line 51) and Carrow's wand-raise (line 53) provide action beats that keep the exchange from reading as transcript.

summary: Single finding addressed. Expanded Amycus Carrow's voiced presence in the corridor Cruciatus scene from one line ('Get up') to four speech acts spanning lines 49-75. Added a between-curses scolding block with high-contraction blunt register and a threat question ('You think I'm thick?'), plus a post-curse dismissal ('Pick it up and go'). All additions are local to the cited span (original lines 41-69), preserve surrounding prose and substrate-analysis passages unchanged, and maintain the scene's existing action choreography and Gavin's observational distance. No changes elsewhere in the chapter.
